channel.title: RSS Aggregator Feed
channel.description: An aggregated feed of Technology-related news

item.title: The end of Moore's Law? Huawei unveils new chip architecture, which it hopes can help it cut the gap on Nvidia and TSMC
item.description: Huawei introduced Tau Scaling Law and LogicFolding architecture as potential alternatives to Moore’s Law and traditional semiconductor scaling limitations.

item.title: Bluesky embraces long-form content to counter X Articles
item.description: In its latest update, Bluesky is getting into long-form content.

item.title: Websites are using this FROST-y new technique to spy on users by snooping on their SSD activity
item.description: A new side-channel attack was discovered but exploiting it is not as easy as it sounds.

item.title: The $6 Billion Chinese Startup Trying to Build Hands for Every Robot
item.description: LinkerBot makes dexterous robotic hands for as little as $600. It wants to become the standard for humanoids and automated factories—and eventually replace human labor altogether.

item.title: Valorisation de 965 milliards | Anthropic annonce lever 65 milliards de dollars
item.description: L’entreprise américaine d’intelligence artificielle (IA) Anthropic, éditrice du modèle Claude, a annoncé jeudi une nouvelle levée de fonds massive de 65 milliards de dollars, portant sa valorisation à 965 milliards, devant son rival OpenAI (852).

item.title: Meta Copies Snapchat’s Homework Again With ‘Plus’ Features for Instagram and Facebook
item.description: Meta’s upcoming Instagram Plus and Facebook Plus subscriptions are the latest example of the company seeing what works elsewhere and mimicking it.

item.title: Hackers are trying to steal Signal users’ backups in new wave of phishing attacks
item.description: A new hacking campaign is trying to trick Signal users to give up their secret recovery key, which can be used to access online backups containing past messages.

item.title: Pentagon awards $9.7 billion in contracts to Dell, Microsoft to provide software, stop license sprawl, and cut costs across military and intelligence networks
item.description: Dell has been awarded a contract to supply Microsoft 365 software and other services for the Department of Defense, the intelligence community, and the US Coast Guard.

item.title: Holy moly! Valve just hiked the Steam Deck OLED price by almost 50% — but I have two fan-favorite handhelds that are still cheap and you should get instead
item.description: ‘Due to rising memory and storage costs’ the Steam Deck now costs over 40% more.

item.title: Anthropic raises $65 Billion, nears $1T valuation ahead of IPO
item.description: Anthropic has closed a $65 billion Series H round at a $965 billion post-money valuation, marking what could be the AI startup's final private fundraise before a highly anticipated IPO.

item.title: How automation has evolved at 24 companies
item.description: Make time for human judgment and higher-level processes by automating the repeatable. Automation and AI aren’t synonymous, but there is a relationship. As AI capabilities have improved, more companies are using it to automate parts of the business that previously were human-touch only. Deciding what to automate and how to use technology for the greatest impact, is something our Fast Company Impact Council members think about daily. Here’s what 24 members said about how they incorporate automation, and how that has changed over time....

item.title: Slate EV truck pre-orders will open on June 24
item.description: Pre-orders are opening up for Slate, the Spirit Airlines of EV trucks

item.title: Motorola’s last-gen Razr Ultra is almost half off
item.description: Motorola’s latest Razr Ultra proves that its flip foldable format has evolved to become more than just a nostalgic gimmick. I’d understand if you’re not interested in shelling out $1,499.99 for the 2026 model, but the similar 2025 Motorola Razr Ultra with 512GB of storage is a much more palatable $699.99 unlocked at Best Buy […]

item.title: Just like gold and oil, we’ll soon be able to trade AI token futures
item.description: Large exchanges are designing derivative products around AI tokens, which are increasingly being considered less a computational output and more a raw material input, like electricity or bandwidth.

item.title: Anthropic's Claude Opus 4.8 is here with 3X cheaper fast mode and near-Mythos level alignment
item.description: Anthropic today released Claude Opus 4.8, an upgrade to its flagship model that ships at the same price as its predecessor, alongside a dramatically cheaper "fast mode" tier and a new feature that lets the model spawn hundreds of parallel subagents for codebase-scale work.The model is available immediately across Anthropic's surfaces — claude.ai, Claude Code, the API, and Cowork — at unchanged pricing: $5 per million input tokens and $25 per million output tokens. Developers can call it as claude-opus-4-8.The headline efficiency story is fast mode. Anthropic has slashed the price of running Op...

item.title: Électricité : être payé pour consommer de l’énergie ? Ce contrat surprenant arrive en France
item.description: Avec l'essor des énergies renouvelables, les prix de l'électricité fluctuent de plus en plus. Et certains fournisseurs ont décidé d'en faire une opportunité, à travers deux offres qui pourraient bientôt s'imposer : la tarification dynamique et le pilotage intelligent.

item.title: Spotify adds playlist folders, bulk editing and background downloads
item.description: Spotify's new features aren't AI slop for a change

item.title: H1 secures $40M from CVS, proving SaaS startups can still attract investment
item.description: CEO Ariel Katz argues that while AI can replicate workflow SaaS, it can't copy H1’s unique doctor data.

item.title: Mars n’a pas toujours été un désert glacé : Curiosity révèle le moment où tout a basculé
item.description: En analysant des roches martiennes étudiées par le rover Curiosity, des chercheurs ont identifié un nouvel indice minéralogique du grand basculement climatique de Mars. La taille des cristaux d’hématite présents dans le cratère Gale suggère que cette région de la planète a connu des conditions...

item.title: The line between games and movies keeps getting blurrier
item.description: The most memorable part of 007 First Light is something that's typically pretty boring: the tutorial. In many games, you're forced through a series of tedious lessons in how to play, presented in a way that feels disconnected from the story itself and at a plodding pace. But First Light does something different. Because the […]

item.title: A security lapse at prison pay phone service Pay Tel publicly exposed over 300K callers’ driver’s licenses
item.description: Pay Tel secured the publicly exposed data after security researchers discovered the leak containing callers' sensitive ID documents and inmate communications.

item.title: I asked ChatGPT to build me a realistic weekly workout for a 54-year-old body — and I actually kept doing it
item.description: I asked ChatGPT to create a realistic home workout for a middle-aged body, and after two weeks, I’m surprised by how effective and sustainable it’s been.

item.title: The LG C6 OLED TV is my dark horse pick for a World Cup winner — I said it ‘sets the bar for TVs in 2026’ in my review — and it’s already been discounted
item.description: The LG C6 has only been around for a few weeks, but we're already seeing some big discounts: perfect timing for the World Cup!

item.title: Wix layoffs today: Tech developer is the latest to cut 20% of jobs while citing AI
item.description: The Israel-based website builder joins a growing list of companies trimming staff amid the ‘fast evolution’ of AI, including Meta, Cisco, and Intuit. Israel-based Wix joins a growing list of companies announcing layoffs and citing “the fast evolution” of AI. (That list includes Meta, Cisco, Groupon, and Intuit, in just the last few weeks.)
